guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Dermatology — Scabies (permethrin 5%, whole-household treatment)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance (dermatology): the source note frames the presentation as an 'urgent', 'agonizing', out-of-hours crisis (explosive nocturnal itch, skin scratched raw and bleeding, a distressed spouse), but the red-flag screen is NEGATIVE — afebrile, systemically well, and explicitly NO honey-coloured crusting or fluctuance to indicate secondary bacterial impetiginisation. The correct disposition is a telehealth/GP-manageable topical scabicide for the whole household plus itch/sleep relief plus environmental decontamination. Over-escalation to ED/000 is the scored defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person after-hours clinician who performed dermatoscopy of the finger-web spaces, examined the genitalia and measured vitals. The AI Doctor cannot examine, dermatoscope, palpate or take vitals, and is scored on recognising the classic infestation triad from the history + video-observable findings, initiating whole-household treatment, and safety-netting.

Contact-tracing / whole-household management: the defining management action is SIMULTANEOUS treatment of every household member regardless of symptoms — tests whether the AI captures the transmission control step, not just the index patient's cream.

Post-scabetic itch counselling: the itch persists for 2-4 weeks after successful treatment; tests that the AI does NOT advise repeated nightly re-application of permethrin (a skin-damaging error of commission) and manages the residual itch with antihistamines instead.

Secondary-infection safety-netting: relentlessly excoriated, bleeding skin carries an impetiginisation/cellulitis risk that cannot be excluded remotely — tests robust, time-bound escalation edges without baseline over-triage.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue); source note DST-035.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
